23:28 â November 10, 2023
Enterprise Fraud Management (EFM) â Decision Mode Updates
We updated information in the EFM Part as described in the table below.
Electronic Transactions Volume
Enterprise Fraud Management Part
Section 2.1, Definitions
Minor updates to a few definitions.
Section 2.2, Frequently Asked Questions
We revised Questions 7, 8, 12, 13. We added Questions 14, 15, 16.
Section 7.7, Using the Activity Filter in an Open Incident
We added a note to clarify that the âUnder investigationâ activity in the Activity filter doesnât apply to Central 1 or credit unions. Selecting it wonât affect any activity filter requests.
Section 8.3, Identifying a Transaction as Legitimate
We updated the images used in this section to add clarity to the process. We also added a note about Auto Risk flagged transactions and how legitimate transactions may require communication with the sender of the
Interac
e-Transfer.
Section 8.4, Identifying Alerts in Auto Risk Transactions
We modified this section to provide more details on Auto Risk transactions.
Section 8.5, Adding Manual Alerts to Unalerted Transactions
We added more details for creating manual alerts in different situations.
Section 8.6, Revising a Previously Identified Alert
This section was moved from its original location and new images were updated to add clarity.
Section 8.7, Using the Search Function
This section was moved from its original location and new images were updated to add clarity.
Section 8.8, Reporting Investigation Results to Central 1
We deleted this section. The connection between OAS and EFM is automated, so you donât need to report investigation results to Central 1 via email.
